title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

seo.title: Pineapple Bun Recipe (Bo Lo Bao) - Easy Homemade Guide
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Chinese Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) recipe at home! Get step-by-step instructions for fluffy buns with a crispy, sweet topping. Perfect for breakfast or snack.

seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked authentic Chinese Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao) with a golden, crispy topping.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the sweet, crispy, and fluffy goodness of homemade Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao), a classic Hong Kong bakery treat!

description: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a beloved Hong Kong bakery classic, known for its soft, fluffy bread and a sweet, crispy, cookie-like topping that resembles the skin of a pineapple. Despite its name, there is no pineapple in the bun; the name refers solely to its distinctive appearance. These delightful buns are perfect for breakfast, a snack, or with a cup of milk tea.

instructions.title: Prepare the Bun Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 250g all-purpose flour, 30g granulated sugar, 4g instant dry yeast, and 3g salt. Add 1 large egg and 100ml warm milk, mixing until a shaggy dough forms. Transfer to a lightly floured surface and knead for 10-12 minutes until smooth and elastic. Incorporate 25g softened unsalted butter and continue kneading until the dough is smooth, pliable, and passes the windowpane test.

instructions.title: First Proofing
instructions.text: Place the kneaded dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover tightly with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until it has visibly doubled in size. This allows the yeast to activate and develop the dough's flavor and texture.

instructions.title: Prepare the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the bun dough is proofing, prepare the topping. In a medium bowl, cream together 60g softened unsalted butter and 60g granulated sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in 1 large egg yolk and 1 tbsp evaporated milk until well combined. Sift in 120g all-purpose flour and 1/2 tsp baking powder, mixing until just combined to form a soft, pliable dough. Divide the topping dough into 8 equal portions and roll each into a smooth ball. Flatten each ball into a disc approximately 3-4 inches in diameter.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the bun dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air. Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and divide it into 8 equal portions, approximately 60-65g each. Roll each portion into a smooth, round bun.

instructions.title: Assemble and Second Proofing
instructions.text: Carefully place one flattened pineapple topping disc over each bun dough ball, molding it gently to cover about two-thirds of the bun's surface. Place the assembled buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, ensuring there is enough space between them for expansion. Loosely cover the buns with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let them proof for another 30-45 minutes, or until they look visibly puffy.

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). In a small bowl, whisk together 1 large egg yolk and 1 tsp water for the egg wash. Just before baking, gently brush the egg wash over the pineapple topping of each bun. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the buns are cooked through. Remove from the oven and let cool slightly on a wire rack before serving warm.

tips: For the crispiest topping, ensure your butter is softened but not melted when making the pineapple topping dough. Overworking the topping dough can make it tough.
If you dont have evaporated milk for the topping, you can substitute with regular whole milk, but evaporated milk gives a slightly richer flavor and smoother texture.
Pineapple Buns are best enjoyed warm on the day they are baked. If you have leftovers, briefly reheat them in a toaster oven for a few minutes to restore some crispiness.
article:
The aroma of freshly baked bread, sweet and buttery, with a hint of something caramelized and crispy – that's the magic of Bo Lo Bao, more famously known as the Pineapple Bun. This iconic treat from Hong Kong bakeries has captured hearts worldwide with its unique texture contrast: a soft, fluffy milk bread base crowned with a sweet, crumbly, cookie-like topping. Despite its name, there's no pineapple fruit in sight; the "pineapple" refers to the beautiful, criss-cross pattern scored into its golden crust. If you've ever dreamt of recreating this beloved delicacy in your own kitchen, you've come to the right place. This comprehensive Pineapple Bun Recipe will guide you through every step to achieve bakery-quality results at home.

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

Bo Lo Bao (菠蘿包), or Pineapple Bun, is a quintessential Hong Kong pastry, a beloved staple in cha chaan tengs (Hong Kong-style diners) and bakeries. Its defining characteristic is the sweet, crunchy, golden-brown crust that sits atop a soft, slightly sweet bread roll. The crust, made from a mixture of flour, sugar, eggs, and butter, is baked until it develops a crackled, checkered appearance, reminiscent of a pineapple's skin – hence the name. The bread underneath is a classic milk bread, known for its incredibly tender and airy crumb. While traditionally served plain, allowing the contrast between the crust and bread to shine, modern versions often include fillings. Mastering this authentic Pineapple Bun Recipe means capturing that delightful textural interplay that makes it so irresistible.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Pineapple Bun involves two main components: the soft bread dough and the signature crunchy topping. Using fresh, quality ingredients is essential for a truly delicious outcome.

**For the Bread Dough:**
*   300g bread flour (high protein flour is best for chewiness)
*   40g granulated sugar
*   3g salt
*   4g active dry yeast
*   160ml warm milk (around 105-115°F / 40-46°C)
*   1 large egg, room temperature
*   30g unsalted butter, softened

**For the Pineapple Topping:**
*   100g all-purpose flour
*   60g granulated sugar
*   60g unsalted butter, softened
*   1/2 large egg (approx. 25-30g), room temperature
*   1/4 teaspoon baking powder (optional, for extra crispiness)
*   1 tablespoon milk powder (optional, for richer flavor)

**For Egg Wash:**
*   1 egg yolk mixed with 1 tablespoon water (for the topping before baking)

Gathering these ingredients is the first step towards a perfect Pineapple Bun Recipe.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Pineapple Buns is a rewarding process that requires a little patience but is entirely achievable for home bakers.

**Part 1: Making the Bread Dough**
1.  **Activate Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the warm milk and a pinch of sugar. Sprinkle the active dry yeast over the top and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy.
2.  **Combine Dry Ingredients:** In a separate bowl, whisk together the bread flour, remaining sugar, and salt.
3.  **Mix Dough:** Add the egg to the yeast mixture, then pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Mix with a spatula or in a stand mixer with a dough hook until a shaggy dough forms.
4.  **Knead:** Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead by hand for 10-15 minutes, or use a stand mixer on medium speed for 8-10 minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic. It should pass the windowpane test (stretch a small piece thinly enough to see light through it without tearing).
5.  **Add Butter:** Gradually add the softened butter, kneading until fully incorporated and the dough is smooth and elastic again.
6.  **First Proofing:** Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size.
7.  **Divide and Shape:** Gently deflate the dough and divide it into 8-10 equal pieces (around 60-70g each). Roll each piece into a smooth ball and place them on a parchment-lined baking sheet, spaced apart.

**Part 2: Making the Pineapple Topping**
1.  **Cream Butter and Sugar:** In a medium bowl, cream together the softened butter and granulated sugar until light and fluffy.
2.  **Add Egg:** Gradually beat in the 1/2 egg until well combined.
3.  **Add Dry Ingredients:** Sift in the all-purpose flour, baking powder (if using), and milk powder (if using). Mix until a soft, uniform dough forms. Do not overmix.
4.  **Chill:** Cover the topping dough with plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to firm up.

**Part 3: Assembling and Baking**
1.  **Prepare Topping for Assembly:** Divide the chilled topping dough into the same number of pieces as your bread dough balls. Roll each piece into a thin disk (about 3-4 inches in diameter) between two sheets of parchment paper or plastic wrap.
2.  **Apply Topping:** Carefully peel a topping disk and place it over a bread dough ball, gently pressing down to adhere and smooth it around the top. The topping should cover about 2/3 to 3/4 of the bun.
3.  **Score:** Using a sharp knife or a dough scraper, gently score a criss-cross pattern onto the top of each bun. Be careful not to cut too deep into the bread dough.
4.  **Second Proofing:** Brush the top of each bun with the egg wash. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let them rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.
5.  **Bake:** Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Bake the Pineapple Buns for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the bread is cooked through.
6.  **Cool:** Transfer the baked buns to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving. Following this detailed Pineapple Bun Recipe will ensure a delicious outcome.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect bakery-style Pineapple Bun at home is all about attention to detail. Here are some pro tips to elevate your Pineapple Bun Recipe:

*   **Dough Hydration:** Don't be afraid to adjust the milk slightly based on your flour and environment. The dough should be soft and slightly sticky after kneading, but not unmanageable.
*   **Kneading is Key:** Thorough kneading develops the gluten, which is crucial for the signature soft and chewy texture of the bread. Don't skimp on this step!
*   **Proofing Environment:** A warm, slightly humid environment is ideal for proofing. You can create one by placing the covered dough in an oven that has been briefly warmed then turned off, or near a bowl of hot water.
*   **Topping Consistency:** The topping dough should be soft enough to roll but firm enough to handle without sticking excessively. Chilling it is vital. If it's too soft, it will melt off the buns; too firm, and it will crack.
*   **Scoring Technique:** Use a very sharp knife and make confident, swift cuts. The scoring creates the "pineapple" pattern and helps the topping spread evenly.
*   **Egg Wash:** The egg wash gives the topping its beautiful golden sheen.
*   **Don't Overbake:** Overbaking can dry out the bread. Keep an eye on the buns; once the topping is golden and the bread sounds hollow when tapped, they're ready. Mastering this Pineapple Bun Recipe truly comes down to practice and these small but significant details.

## Serving Suggestions

Pineapple Buns are incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways, making them a perfect treat for any time of day.

*   **Classic "Bo Lo Yau":** The most iconic way to enjoy a Pineapple Bun is fresh from the oven, sliced in half horizontally, and tucked with a thick slab of cold, unsalted butter. The butter melts into the warm bread, creating a rich, creamy contrast to the crispy, sweet topping.
*   **With Beverages:** They pair wonderfully with a cup of Hong Kong-style milk tea, coffee, or a simple hot tea for a traditional afternoon tea experience.
*   **Breakfast or Snack:** Enjoy them plain as a satisfying breakfast item or a delightful snack.
*   **Dessert Twist:** For a modern take, serve a warm Pineapple Bun with a scoop of vanilla ice cream, letting the ice cream melt slightly into the soft bread.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Pineapple Bun is a masterpiece in itself, there are many ways to customize this Pineapple Bun Recipe to suit different tastes or dietary needs.

*   **Fillings:**
    *   **Custard (Nai Wong Bao):** A sweet, creamy custard filling is a popular choice.
    *   **Red Bean Paste:** A traditional Asian dessert filling, offering a subtly sweet, earthy flavor.
    *   **BBQ Pork (Cha Siu Bao):** For a savory twist, a sweet and savory BBQ pork filling is surprisingly delicious with the sweet crust.
    *   **Coconut:** A mixture of shredded coconut, sugar, and butter makes for a fragrant and chewy filling.
*   **Topping Flavors:** Add a touch of vanilla extract, almond extract, or citrus zest (lemon or orange) to the topping for an extra layer of flavor.
*   **Dough Enrichment:** For an even richer bread, you can add an extra egg yolk to the dough mixture.
*   **Dietary Adjustments:**
    *   **Dairy-Free:** Substitute milk with a plant-based milk (like almond or soy) and butter with a good quality dairy-free butter alternative. Note that textures might vary slightly.
    *   **Gluten-Free:** While challenging to replicate the exact texture, you can experiment with gluten-free bread flour blends, though significant adjustments to liquid and kneading may be required.
    *   Experimenting with your Pineapple Bun Recipe is part of the fun!

The journey of baking your own Pineapple Buns is incredibly rewarding. From the satisfying process of kneading the dough to the moment you pull those golden, fragrant buns from the oven, every step is a delight. The contrast of the crispy, sweet topping with the pillowy-soft bread is an experience that transports you straight to a bustling Hong Kong bakery. Don't be intimidated; with this detailed guide, you have everything you need to succeed. So, roll up your sleeves and try this classic Pineapple Bun Recipe today – your taste buds will thank you!
